input.title: CRM System
input.description: CRM System is a web-based enterprise platform that centralizes operational workflows, data visibility, and cross-functional reporting.

user_classes.Admin: Configures the system, manages users, and oversees permissions and settings.
user_classes.Manager: Oversees departmental workflows, approvals, KPIs, and operational reporting.
user_classes.Employee: Performs day-to-day operational tasks within assigned modules and permissions.
user_classes.User: Generic authenticated platform user with access limited by role and department.

definitions.Admin: System user with full administrative privileges.
definitions.Manager: Operational owner of a department, module, or workflow.
definitions.Employee: End user performing role-specific business operations.

constraints: Initial release will focus on the web platform only
Role-based access control is mandatory across all modules
Operational data must be auditable and retained according to policy

operating_environment.frontend: Web-based interface built with React and accessible through modern browsers.
operating_environment.backend: Service layer implemented with Python Flask, PostgreSQL for business operations.
operating_environment.deployment: Cloud-hosted environment with centralized monitoring, backups, and automated deployments.

NFR-01.title: Security and Access Control
requirements: All sensitive data must be encrypted in transit and at rest
Role-based authorization must restrict access by module and action
All authentication events and privileged actions must be logged

NFR-02.title: Performance and Scalability
requirements: Core pages and dashboards should load within acceptable enterprise SLAs
The platform must support concurrent usage across multiple departments
Background jobs and reporting workloads should not block transactional workflows

NFR-03.title: Reliability and Recoverability
requirements: Automated backups must run on a defined schedule
Critical workflows should support retry-safe processing and audit logging
Operational alerts should be generated for failures and degraded services

system_attributes.reliability: Platform should provide resilient transactional processing with backup and recovery capabilities.
system_attributes.scalability: Architecture should support horizontal scaling for APIs, background workers, and reporting services.
system_attributes.security: Authentication, authorization, encryption, and auditability must be built into every module.
system_attributes.maintainability: Codebase should remain modular, observable, and safe to extend through clear service boundaries.

out_of_scope: Native mobile applications in the first release
Large third-party marketplace or legacy ERP migrations unless explicitly scoped
Non-core experimental modules outside the platform's operational workflows

FR-01.title: Contact Management
requirements: Add contacts with name, email, phone, company

FR-02.title: Deal Pipeline
requirements: Create deals with value, stage, and close date
Multiple pipelines for different sales processes

FR-03.title: Activity Tracking
requirements: Activity timeline per contact and deal

FR-05.title: Task Management
requirements: Create tasks with due date and assignee

FR-07.title: Lead Scoring
requirements: Score leads based on configurable criteria
Score decay over time for inactivity

FR-08.title: Workflow Automation
requirements: Create automated workflows with triggers and actions
Triggers: deal stage change, new contact, score threshold
Actions: send email, create task, update field, notify

FR-09.title: Company Management
requirements: Create company records with industry and size

FR-10.title: Dashboard and Analytics
requirements: Key metrics: total deals, revenue, conversion rate
Export data to CSV and PDF

FR-01.title: Plan Contact Management Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for contact management and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Add contacts with name, email, phone, company; Import contacts from CSV; Tag and categorize contacts.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for contact management is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for contact management are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for contact management can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Contact Management Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for contact management on React, Python Flask, PostgreSQL. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Add contacts with name, email, phone, company; Import contacts from CSV; Tag and categorize contacts.
acceptance_criteria: Core contact management business rules are implemented for CRM System
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for contact management
API/service behavior for contact management is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Contact Management Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for contact management with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The contact management user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for contact management behave correctly
The contact management UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Design Contact Management Persistence Model
FR-01.description: Design the persistence layer for contact management. Identify the core entities, relationships, indexes, status fields, history/audit tables, and reporting attributes required to support the workflow. Add migrations and seed or fixture data where that helps engineering validate the feature early.
acceptance_criteria: The persistence model for contact management supports required workflows and reporting needs
Indexes, constraints, and relationships for contact management are defined correctly
Migrations for contact management can be applied safely in target environments

FR-01.title: Integrate External Dependencies for Contact Management
FR-01.description: Implement the external integration points used by contact management, such as third-party APIs, SSO, messaging gateways, payment rails, import/export pipelines, or bank feeds. Add retry-safe behavior, timeout handling, observability, and fallback behavior for downstream failures.
acceptance_criteria: External dependencies used by contact management are connected through stable integration layers
Timeout, retry, and failure handling for contact management integrations are implemented and testable
Secrets, credentials, and integration logs for contact management are handled securely

FR-01.title: Write Contact Management Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for contact management. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for contact management are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for contact management are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical contact management scenarios
